topic: horror sound effects when useful CapCut SFX mixing
niche: Don't Blink — terrifying faceless horror Shorts (~30s). Finale scare or suspense-replay.
viewer_moment: Viewer is in recreational fear — safe but vigilant — during false calm; amygdala primed for startle at finale.
emotional_stakes: Wrong SFX (mid-video stingers, long static, loud under VO) breaks immersion and causes mutes/skips; right SFX multiplies earned scares.
hook_angles: Single diegetic alert at 0s — cam motion ping, not a stinger
Strip audio at false calm so the finale hit feels 2x louder
CapCut: three lanes — VO / SFX / ambience — stinger only last 2s on finale drafts
script_beats: Hook 0-3s: one diegetic wrong-detail SFX (notification, cam beep)
Pattern 3-12s: micro UI/room cues per cut, under VO
Escalation 12-20s: acousmatic foley (creak, footsteps, static) — no jump sting
False calm 20-26s: remove layers — room tone or silence (contrast)
Finale profile: fast-attack stinger ~2s before end + visual sync
Suspense-replay profile: fade hold, no stinger — replay bait
visual_framing: Mute-safe visuals mandatory; SFX reinforces sync'd cuts. Place CapCut SFX 1-3 frames before visual peak.
competitor_gap: Generic horror Shorts stack loud layers entire video and skip false-calm contrast; miss diegetic specificity (cam/UI) for pillar hooks.
quality_notes: Credible sources required: peer-reviewed jump-scare studies (Frontiers 2025), Brill ASE trailer analysis, Kerins/Redfern on silence-before-loud. CapCut workflow is Tier C craft only.

web_sources.title: (Re)Considering the jump scare in four elements
web_sources.snippet: Baseline → anticipation → stinger arrival → return; integrated soundtrack includes SFX and silence.

web_sources.title: Acoustic Startle Events in Trailers for US Horror Films
web_sources.snippet: 173 ASEs; silence/quiet before many events; sound+edit+motion interrelationship.

web_sources.title: What makes a film score frightening?
web_sources.snippet: Dissonance, tremolo, drones, stingers as primal bursts; silence heightens Psycho-style attacks.

web_sources.title: How jump scares work: art and biology of the startle
web_sources.snippet: Auditory dominance in startle; silence in buildup increases contrast.

web_sources.title: CapCut audio: voice first, ducking, SFX levels
web_sources.snippet: VO priority; SFX for emphasis; manual/auto ducking under speech.

recommended_path: Read HORROR_SOUND_EFFECTS_RESEARCH.md → open pack CAPCUT_SFX.md → CapCut: import VO + clips → SFX lane per beat → duck music → export. Pipeline still renders base MP4 via ffmpeg; CapCut pass adds SFX polish.
search_queries: horror film sound design jump scare stinger academic
acoustic startle events horror trailers Redfern
CapCut add sound effects ducking voiceover

credible_source_policy.tier_A: peer-reviewed journals, academic theses, DOI papers

capcut_workflow_summary.finale_stinger: fast attack no fade-in max 0.3s at total_duration minus 2.5s
